revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岩屋古墳 (小牧市)]][[zh:岩屋古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Iwaya kofun-01.jpg|250px]] | caption = Restored tumulus | location = {{ill|Komaki|qid=Q389537|1=Komaki}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.313172222222|N|136.93510833333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built = Early 7th century | burial = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | area = Approx. 30m diameter (restored size) | Interred_individual = | burial goods = Earrings, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, Yamachawan, etc. | Historic_site = | notes = Relocated and restored | map = Japan Aichi | map = | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = bottom | architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }} '''Iwaya Kofun''' (岩屋古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|qid=Q389537|lt=Komaki|1=Komaki}}, Aichi Prefecture.
== Overview == The tumulus was originally situated in a bamboo grove near {{ill|Kōtō-ji|ja|杲洞寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtō-ji|1=Kōdō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11528794}}, at the southeastern foot of {{ill|Iwasakiyama (Aichi Prefecture)|ja|岩崎山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasakiyama (Aichi Prefecture)|1=iwasakiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11474475}}. Constructed during the late [[Kofun period]] (late 6th to early 7th century), the site was excavated between August and December 2007 (Heisei 19) due to urban development<ref name="report-3">Excavation Report, p.3</ref>. Following the investigation, the tumulus was relocated and restored at Iwasakiyama Park, northeast of its original location. [[ja:岩屋古墳 (小牧市)]]
== burial and area == [[File:Iwaya_kofun-03.jpg|thumb|200px|Interior view of the stone chamber from the passage]] The mound's original structure was heavily damaged, with most of the northwest-facing portion lost<ref name="report-5">Excavation Report, p.5</ref>. Based on detected ditch-like remains around the site, the tumulus diameter is estimated to have been approximately 20–25 meters{{R|report-5}}.
Significant erosion occurred on the south and west sides, exposing stone materials from the passage. On the eastern side, the mound was entirely removed, exposing the eastern section of the stone chamber{{R|report-5}}. This destruction is believed to have occurred during the Edo period to create access to a stone quarry located behind the tumulus{{R|report-5}}.
The burial chamber is a pseudo-double-sleeved {{ill|yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}<ref name="report-6">Excavation Report, p.6</ref>. Key measurements include: * burial chamber (胴張型玄室): Length 3.8m, width 2–2.2m, height 2.2m. * Doorway: Width 1.2m, height 1.8m. * Passage (羨道): Remaining length 3.6m, width 1.9m. The chamber is constructed from granite sourced from Iwasakiyama<ref name="data-1">Data Compilation 1 (Historic Site Section), p.18</ref>. The burial chamber walls feature three to four courses of overlapping stones in a corbelling style<ref name="report-27">Excavation Report, p.27</ref>. Large stones were used, with base stones measuring around 1m, a mirror stone approximately 1.5m wide, and a doorway stone up to 2m long, including the buried section{{R|report-6}}. Chisel marks from the original construction are visible on some stones{{R|report-6}}.
Among the finds are parts of a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} lid, though much of the sarcophagus was lost, likely removed for use as building material<ref name="report-28_30">Excavation Report, pp.28–30</ref>. The estimated sarcophagus length is 2m{{R|report-27}}. The stone material, tuff with gravel inclusions, is believed to have been transported from the Zenshino area near modern-day {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|qid=Q503411|lt=Inuyama|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}<ref name="report-30">Excavation Report, p.30</ref>.
Below the mound, remains of four pit dwellings, seven ditch-like features, and over 20 pits were uncovered<ref name="report-16">Excavation Report, pp.16–17</ref>.
== burial goods == === burial goods === * [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]] (gold rings) * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (long-necked jars, pedestalled dishes, flat bottles)
=== Other Finds === * Stone tools (arrowheads, axes) * Cord-marked pottery (deep bowls, jars) * {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|qid=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}} (jars, urns, pedestalled dishes) * {{ill|Gray-glazed pottery|ja|灰釉陶器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gray-glazed pottery|1=Gray-glazed pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48758373}} (bowls) * {{ill|Yamachawan|ja|山茶碗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamachawan|1=yama-chawan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48760183}} * {{ill|Old Seto ware|ja|古瀬戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Seto ware|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古瀬戸様式|1=Old Seto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11411456}} (four-handled jars)
Grave goods date the tumulus construction to the early 7th century{{R|report-30}}. The presence of Yamachawan near the floor suggests that tomb looting began in the early 13th century{{R|report-27}}.
== Restoration Project == [[File:Iwaya_kofun-04.jpg|thumb|200px|Chisel marks on a ceiling stone]] Following excavation, Iwaya Kofun was disassembled and relocated to Iwasakiyama Park, where it was reconstructed as a 30m-diameter {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/Files/1/00000021/attach/ajiokachiku-4kaihennkou.pdf |title=Urban Redevelopment Plan (4th Revision) Ajioka District |publisher=Komaki City |date=2009-07-22 |accessdate=2012-05-13 }}</ref>. Missing stone chamber materials were supplemented during restoration, and the mound was rebuilt, except for the top, which was left open to allow observation of the chamber's ceiling stones. Steps were a11ed for easier access. Chisel marks on the ceiling stones suggest they were quarried during the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} era for the construction of [[Nagoya Castle]]<ref name="report-28">Excavation Report, pp.28–29</ref>.
== Access == ''See: {{ill|Iwasakiyama (Aichi Prefecture)#Access|ja|岩崎山 (愛知県)#交通手段|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasakiyama (Aichi Prefecture)#Access|1=Mount Iwasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11474476}}''
== References == * Komaki City Historical Committee, ''Komaki City History: Data Compilation 1 (Historic Site Section),'' Komaki City, 1975. * ''Iwaya Kofun Excavation Report,'' Komaki City Board of Education, 2008.
== notes ==
{{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505809}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Iwaya kofun (Komaki)}} * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/komakitoyoyama.html Ancient burial Research Office – Kofun in Komaki and Toyoyama, Aichi Prefecture] (includes pre-excavation photographs)
{{Kofun navbar}}
